howItWorks.intro: This trig function evaluator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What are the six trigonometric functions?
faq.answer: The six trig functions are sine (sin), cosine (cos), tangent (tan), cosecant (csc = 1/sin), secant (sec = 1/cos), and cotangent (cot = 1/tan or cos/sin).

faq.question: When is tangent undefined?
faq.answer: Tangent is undefined when the cosine equals zero, which occurs at 90°, 270°, or more generally at 90° + 180°n for any integer n. At these angles, the ratio sin/cos involves division by zero.

faq.question: What is the difference between degrees and radians?
faq.answer: Degrees divide a full circle into 360 parts; radians use the ratio of arc length to radius. One full circle = 360° = 2π radians. To convert: radians = degrees × π/180.

faq.question: What are the reciprocal trig functions?
faq.answer: Cosecant (csc) is the reciprocal of sine, secant (sec) is the reciprocal of cosine, and cotangent (cot) is the reciprocal of tangent. For example, csc(θ) = 1/sin(θ).
